ogTitle: Bonny Hills vs Bonnyrigg Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bonny Hills and Bonnyrigg Heights.
heading: Bonny Hills vs Bonnyrigg Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bonny Hills, New South Wales and Bonnyrigg Heights, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bonny Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Bonnyrigg Heights (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bonny Hills or Bonnyrigg Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bonny Hills has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Bonnyrigg Heights scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bonny Hills scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Bonnyrigg Heights scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bonny Hills or Bonnyrigg Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bonny Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
